summary:
Combining metoprolol and verapamil increases the risk of bradyarrhythmias such as sinus bradycardia and second-degree atrioventricular block due to their combined negative chronotropic effect. This results from multiple studies showing additive cardiovascular risks associated with using both medications together. Summary focusing only on key clinical points:

Combining metoprolol and verapamil heightens the likelihood of bradyarrhythmias like sinus bradycardia and second-degree heart blocks because they have a synergistic negative impact on heart rate regulation. Numerous studies confirm increased cardiovascular risks when these two drugs are used concurrently.
